skill.name: brandkit
skill.description: Generates premium brand-kit image prompts and visual direction for identity boards, logo systems, mockups, palettes, typography, and brand-world presentations.
skill.summary: Create polished brand-kit image direction for identity systems and presentation boards.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command, sensitive-data, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only markdown design guidance and inline formatting examples. No executable code, network access, secret access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Layout Notation Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown backtick text as Ruby or shell execution. The cited lines describe grid sizes and monogram examples for image composition, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is markdown inline code used for layout labels and logo concept examples. No shell command syntax or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive Windows SAM Database Match
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged a sensitive Windows SAM database pattern at a heading. The line reads as a brand-board panel-system heading and contains no credential, database, or operating-system access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a markdown heading for a default 3 by 3 panel system. It does not reference Windows accounts, registry data, or local files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Collisions
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography detections are substring matches inside design vocabulary such as designer, desired, overdesigned, slides, and design. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, encryption code, or security configuration appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are prose about brand design and presentation quality. There is no code path that uses DES, MD5, SHA1, or another weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Design Terms
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are design-system and grid terminology inside a brand identity guide. The skill does not instruct the model to inspect hostnames, processes, users, files, environment variables, or network state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe visual systems, grids, and layout structure. No reconnaissance commands, APIs, or host-inspection intent were found.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear brand visuals before investing in full identity production. This skill turns brand strategy into structured image-generation direction for polished brand-kit boards.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured prompts for premium brand-kit overview images.
Guides logo concept direction using category, audience, and metaphor.
Defines board layouts such as 3 by 3, 2 by 3, and compact concept grids.
Specifies visual modes for developer tools, security, editorial, luxury, and consumer brands.
Describes panels for logo covers, construction, mockups, palettes, typography, and image direction.
Applies anti-generic rules to reduce clutter, copied marks, and weak presentation boards.
limitations: It provides image-generation direction, not final editable vector logo files.
It cannot verify trademark availability or legal originality of a mark.
It depends on the image model to render text and logos accurately.
It does not replace a full brand strategy workshop or production design system.

use_cases.title: Launch Direction for Founders
use_cases.description: Create a polished first brand world for a startup, product, or pitch before hiring a full design team.

use_cases.title: Concept Boards for Designers
use_cases.description: Generate structured visual routes that compare logo ideas, layout systems, mockups, and brand atmosphere.

use_cases.title: Campaign Identity Exploration
use_cases.description: Build presentation-ready brand boards for launches, editorial concepts, gaming brands, or developer-tool identities.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Brand Kit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a brand name and basic category.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a premium brand-kit overview image for [brand name]. The category is [category]. Use a clean 3 by 3 grid, a simple logo idea, refined colors, and minimal typography.

prompt_templates.title: Strategy-Led Identity Board
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you know audience, positioning, and tone.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand-kit image for [brand name]. Audience: [audience]. Positioning: [positioning]. Personality: [traits]. Core metaphor: [metaphor]. Show logo cover, construction, digital mockup, palette, typography, and image direction.

prompt_templates.title: Visual Mode Exploration
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when comparing a specific visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [dark developer, security, luxury, editorial, or consumer] brand-kit board for [brand name]. Use disciplined layout, strong gutters, consistent logo usage, and a palette that supports [desired mood].

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Brand System Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for a detailed board with explicit system rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a presentation-ready brand-system board for [brand name]. Include a symbolic logo concept based on [metaphor], a [layout] grid, product UI application, construction logic, campaign image direction, typography hierarchy, physical touchpoint, and strict anti-generic restraint.

output_examples.input: A developer tool named Framebolt that helps teams ship interface changes faster.
output_examples.output: A dark developer brand board with a bolt-and-frame logo, terminal-inspired UI panel, cyan accent color, construction grid, and launch-ready product mockup.

output_examples.input: A premium privacy compliance platform for legal and operations teams.
output_examples.output: A light editorial identity board with seal-based logo logic, refined paper textures, document mockups, calm typography, and restrained blue and gold accents.

output_examples.input: A gaming analytics product with a fast and competitive personality.
output_examples.output: A high-contrast brand-kit board with a signal-based symbol, dashboard application, vivid accent system, trophy-like details, and controlled campaign imagery.

best_practices: Provide the brand category, audience, personality, and one core metaphor.
Choose one visual mode and one disciplined palette before generating.
Ask for consistent logo repetition across mockups, construction, and application panels.
anti_patterns: Do not request a generic logo without strategy, audience, or symbolic direction.
Do not ask for many unrelated palettes, symbols, and layouts in one image.
Do not use the output as legal proof that a logo is original or trademark-safe.

faq.question: Does this skill create final production logo files?
faq.answer: No. It creates image-generation direction and visual boards. A designer should recreate final marks as editable vector assets.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the brand name, category, audience, personality, core metaphor, preferred layout, and desired visual mode.

faq.question: Can it match a reference image?
faq.answer: It can use references for quality, rhythm, layout, and mood. It should not copy exact content or famous logos.

faq.question: Is it safe for security-sensitive work?
faq.answer: The skill contains design guidance only. It does not request secrets, run commands, or access systems.

faq.question: What makes the output different from a moodboard?
faq.answer: It asks for a complete identity system with logo logic, applications, typography, color, imagery, and presentation rhythm.
